task.title: Phase 3W Exercise: Publishing, Filtering, and Multi-Variant Builds
task.shortdesc: Build HTML5 and PDF output, apply DITAVAL filtering to create audience-specific variants, and configure build properties. Primary track for technical writers.

ul.li: Completed Phase 2 exercises (TaskFlow docs with map and reuse)
Read Part 5 (Making Content Intelligent) and Part 6 (Publishing) topics

context.p: In this exercise you will learn how to produce different output formats, filter content for different audiences, and create repeatable build configurations. These are the daily skills of a DITA publishing workflow.

step.cmd: Build HTML5 output with full sidebar navigation.

info.p: The --nav-toc=full flag embeds a table of contents sidebar.

stepresult.p: Open out/taskflow-html5/index.html. You should see a left sidebar with your topichead groups and all topics listed.

step.cmd: Add audience profiling attributes to your TaskFlow topics.

info.p: Add audience attributes to specific content:

ul.li: In what-is-taskflow.dita, add a section with audience="admin" for administrative features
In cli-commands.dita, add rows with audience="admin" for admin-only commands
In installing-taskflow.dita, add a step with audience="developer" for developer SDK setup

info.codeblock:
Admin Features

TaskFlow administrators can manage teams, configure permissions, and view audit logs.
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <val> <prop action="exclude" att="audience" val="admin"/> <prop action="exclude" att="audience" val="developer"/> </val>
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <val> <prop action="include" att="audience" val="admin"/> <prop action="exclude" att="audience" val="developer"/> </val>

step.cmd: Build two audience-specific variants of your documentation.

info.codeblock:
# End-user variant (no admin content) dita -i taskflow-docs/taskflow.ditamap -f html5 \ -o out/taskflow-enduser \ --filter=taskflow-docs/filters/end-user.ditaval \ --nav-toc=full
# Admin variant (includes admin content) dita -i taskflow-docs/taskflow.ditamap -f html5 \ -o out/taskflow-admin \ --filter=taskflow-docs/filters/admin.ditaval \ --nav-toc=full

stepresult.p: Compare the two outputs side by side:

ul.li: End-user output should NOT show admin sections or admin CLI commands
Admin output should include admin content but exclude developer content

step.cmd: Create a properties file for repeatable builds.

info.codeblock: args.input = taskflow.ditamap output.dir = out/taskflow-enduser transtype = html5 args.filter = filters/end-user.ditaval nav-toc = full
info.p: Now you can build with a single flag:

stepresult.p: Same output as before, but now the build is reproducible and can be committed to version control.

step.cmd: Build PDF output from the same source.

info.note: PDF output requires the default PDF plugin. If the build fails, check that you have a DITA-OT distribution that includes it (most do).

stepresult.p: Open out/taskflow-pdf/taskflow.pdf. Content comes from the same source as HTML5 — this is single-source, multi-channel publishing.

step.cmd: Add metadata to improve content findability.

info.p: Add a <prolog> section to each topic with:
Rebuild and check HTML output — DITA-OT uses metadata for page titles, search indexing, and navigation breadcrumbs.

p.b: Self-Check — you should now be able to:

ul.li: ✓ Build HTML5 and PDF from the same DITA source
✓ Add audience profiling attributes to control content visibility
✓ Create DITAVAL filter files for different audiences
✓ Build audience-specific variants from one source
✓ Use properties files for repeatable builds
✓ Add metadata for findability and indexing
